title: Bridge Essay: Behavioral, Neural, and Computational Principles of Bodily Self-Consciousness.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Birth/Body** (Phase 1) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Birth/Body to mechanistic neuroscience through Behavioral,, Neural,, and, Computational, Principles.

**Behavioral, Neural, and Computational Principles of Bodily Self-Consciousness.** — Neuron. PMID: 26447578. DOI: 10.1016/j.neuron.2015.09.029. Recent work in human cognitive neuroscience has linked self-consciousness to the processing of multisensory bodily signals (bodily self-consciousness [BSC]) in fronto-parietal cortex and more posterior temporo-parietal regions. We highlight the behavioral, neurophysiological, neuroimaging, and computational laws that subtend BSC in humans and non-human primates. We propose that BSC includes body-centered perception (hand, face, and trunk), based on the integration of proprioceptive, vestibular,
